title: Cold & Flu Season Bundle: Parcetamol, Ibuprofen, Diclo, Nasal Decon, Cold & Flu $25.99 Delivered @PharmacySavings
body:
Deal: Cold & Flu Season Bundle: Parcetamol, Ibuprofen, Diclo, Nasal Decon, Cold & Flu $25.99 Delivered @PharmacySavings

[BestTechAdvisor] Last order came quickly. Grabbed this order too.
[BestTechAdvisor] My last order was antihistamines, so not exactly related to this order.
[cloudie9] Hi OP, any loperamide hydrochloride med on specials?
[speedyjonzalas] Save youself time and dont bother ordering any of this stuff. Just get pseudoephedrine. Yes you need to provide ID and have to order it from the counter but its the only thing that does anything for decongestant.
That along with a mix of hydrolytes, paracetamol and ibuprofen from aldi is about is good as it gets.
[Jackson] Yeah I don't know why this phelylxxx is still on sale. It's been reported for years now that it's broken down before it gets to your blood stream and so can't be effective. The new ingredient in these is for coughs and may or may not work.
OP can you swap the Trust out for pseudoepedrine if we provide ID?
[Save Medicare] I don't know why the TGA still allows oral phenylephrine to be marketed as a nasal decongestant at this point. The evidence against its efficacy is overwhelming, and patients are just wasting their money. The US FDA has proposed removing it from the market for this indication.
Topical intranasal phenylephrine gets used frequently in hospitals as a periprocedural vasoconstrictor. Intravenous phenylephrine I have never seen used in Australia, but is more commonly used overseas.
[JinXR] Agreed - phenylephrine should be taken off the market. You can still get pseudoephradrine behind the counter (with your drivers licence).
We get a packet of pseudoephradrine by itself, and take it that way. Actually does what it is supposed to.
[Domingo] Full disclosure to keep the Ozb trolls at bay, this bundle includes 1x item which contains 1x ingredient (Phenylephrine hydrochloride) which is generally considered ineffective as a nasal decongestant when consumed orally. If the 1x ingredient in the 1x item in the bundle makes you angry with rage and hatred towards me, this bundle may not be for you. This bundle also includes a Nasal Decongestant Spray which is considered highly effective.
It's a bit rich calling people trolls for pointing out the significant evidence that the active ingredient in a product you're pushing is ineffective at what it is marketed to do.
[jason101] I don't want to get people off side - but we have thousands of clients buying this product annually, 1x active ingredient of many in one of the items in this bundle is generally accepted as ineffective, a very small handful of Ozbargainers make it their life work to go to extreme lengths to make sure the 99% of clients actually wanting this product have no ability to access it as any rep poster knows if they advertise the product they will be flamed to within an inch of their lives, the result is the 99% miss out as Cold and Flu tabs are never advertised, irrespective of how good the "deal" is.
I have pointed out the products known deficiencies and provided a substitute product in the bundle which can provide nasal congestion relief.
I have literally received hate mail over posting Cold and Flu meds in deals gone by. People telling me they will boycott all of my deals, neg me into oblivion, never buy from me again, warn others away from me (and far, far worse) - yet we also receive many requests for the product. Its the top seller in our store at this time of year, I use it myself, my family uses it, does 1x of the active ingredients do nothing = probably, does this mean the overall deal should not be posted?
I am not calling people out for making their opinions known, I'm not even disputing the opinions, but any long time Ozbargainer knows, once one or two people get on the bandwagon, it's on for young and old - I do think some of the lurkers around the place are sitting in a dark corner waiting to pounce, just ask the last person who posted a bottled water deal, or a soda-stream deal.
[Jackson] Sorry you have had to cop it, maybe if you can sub that item for people with something they want as an option then it would put the ball in their court and you would sound more reasonable to those of us that are unreasonable?
Some options could be to subbing pseudo as I requested above, or give a second nasal one, or an option of one other medication. The main thing I miss is a nasal decongestant that I can use at night to sleep better, currently I just use paracetamol, but something a bit better would be good. As much as I need something like that, I can't see why anyone would take a medication that had ingredients that they knew were ineffective.
I also think that many people rely on their pharmacist to recommend medications and by making the list and later finding out it's main active ingredient is ineffective it may make them feel that they are missing out on that advice
